name: Network Traffic Flow
description: Summarized network packet data that captures session-level details such as source/destination IPs, ports, protocol types, timestamps, and data volume, without storing full packet payloads. This is commonly used for traffic analysis, anomaly detection, and network performance monitoring.

protocols.name: NetFlow
protocols.description: Summarized metadata for network conversations (no packet payloads).

protocols.name: sFlow
protocols.description: Captures sampled packets from switches and routers; used for real-time monitoring.

windows.description: Logs process-level network activity; detects malicious outbound connections.

linux.description: Monitors system calls for network connections.

aws.description: Captures metadata for traffic between EC2 instances, security groups, and internet gateways.

azure_gcp.description: Logs ingress/egress traffic for cloud-based resources.
